item.title: P&G warns of $1bn hit from Iran war after failing to hedge oil exposure
item.description: US consumer goods group said it may raise prices as conflict drives up costs of fuel and packaging

item.title: Big Four’s shrinkage at the top presages weakness at the bottom line
item.description: Professional service firms appear to be braced for flatlining growth

item.title: Why do I have so much in UK equities?
item.description: British pension schemes will soon be forced to support my portfolio

item.title: How quickly can Gulf oil start gushing again?
item.description: Schrodinger’s Strait

item.title: Lotus unveils plug-in hybrid in latest luxury car pivot away from electric
item.description: British sports-car maker to launch Chinese-made Eletre X in Europe in June

item.title: Collapsed Century Capital lacked approvals, administrators find
item.description: Report into bridging lender finds 2 vehicles did not have anti-money laundering registrations

item.title: Is it time to pave over London’s golf courses?
item.description: Often underused green spaces are being targeted as a creative solution for sclerotic housebuilding rates in the capital. But environmentalists are calling the plan a duff

item.title: White House accuses China of ‘industrial-scale’ theft of AI technology
item.description: Trump official Michael Kratsios says Chinese entities stealing from American labs

item.title: Intel shares set to jump 28% to record high on AI sales boom
item.description: CEO says US chipmaker has made ‘fundamental’ changes after year-long turnaround

item.title: ECB steps up its challenge to Visa and Mastercard
item.description: Central bank picks technology rules that will pave way for digital euro by 2029

item.title: The Americas’ Fastest-Growing Companies
item.description: The FT’s seventh annual ranking by compound annual revenue growth. Plus: how listed companies overcome obstacles; AI and defence start-ups dominate investment; the US and China in Latin America; and technology shakes up Canadian wealth management

item.title: Is my SUV large enough?
item.description: Too large. Too ugly. Too dangerous. But can I really afford not to go big?

item.title: Therapy company mixes emotional and artificial intelligence to top ranking
item.description: Grow Therapy heads The Americas’ Fastest-Growing Companies 2026 list while testing AI’s potential

item.title: The companies thriving in turbulent times for IT and software
item.description: Innovative businesses in the Americas can do well despite recent upheaval

item.title: AI and defence companies dominate US growth investment
item.description: Investors show ‘insatiable appetite’ for the sectors while others struggle for funding

item.title: Innovative tech shakes up wealth management in Canada
item.description: Advances in digital tools are bringing services once limited to the ultra-wealthy to more customers

item.title: How The Americas’ Fastest-Growing Companies find a way to thrive
item.description: Ranking highlights businesses that overcome challenges from political shifts to inflation and war

item.title: Green fertiliser project challenges industry’s reliance on natural gas
item.description: UK-listed Atome to develop project in Paraguay that uses hydropower to replace need for fossil fuel

item.title: British retail sales rose 0.7% in March as motorists stocked up on petrol
item.description: Better than expected figure underlines impact of Middle East conflict

item.title: Porsche sells stake in Bugatti owner
item.description: German sports-car maker scales back EV ambitions under new boss Michael Leiters

item.title: Cohere and Aleph Alpha agree $20bn transatlantic AI tie-up
item.description: Canadian and German start-ups to focus on ‘sovereign’ AI systems independent of US and China

item.title: MPS chief considers €7.4bn Generali stake sale
item.description: The proceeds would help fund tie-up between Monte dei Paschi di Siena and Banco BPM

item.title: The dollar swap puzzle
item.description: Plus, where’s the love for clean energy?

item.title: Strategy’s teetering financial tower
item.description: A bitcoin price reprieve can’t erase the company’s contradictions

item.title: FirstFT: Poland’s Donald Tusk casts doubt on US defence pledge to Europe
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Vanguard boss warns about prediction markets and Intel shares surge in after-hours trading

item.title: Banks charged sharply different fees for access to Anthropic investment
item.description: Disparate pricing raises prospect of investors earning unequal returns in the same deal on the basis of who their banker is

item.title: How Swiss concessions failed to ease UBS’s $20bn capital concerns
item.description: Switzerland’s Federal Council this week laid out a compromise which set up another fight with the country’s biggest bank

item.title: JD Sports chair quit after failing to convince board to oust CEO
item.description: Andy Higginson stepped down this week after pushing for CEO Régis Schultz to be replaced

item.title: Europe’s M&A dreams face a German political test
item.description: Large mergers in Germany could create ‘European champions’ — but will Berlin stand aside?

item.title: Rightwing media mogul rattles France’s literary scene
item.description: Writers flee Paris publishing house Grasset in protest at conservative owner Vincent Bolloré
